channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Webb telescope detects carbon dioxide, hydrogen peroxide on Pluto’s Charon for the first time
item.description: Observations by the James Webb Space Telescope are giving scientists a fuller understanding about the composition and evolution of Pluto’s moon Charon, the largest moon orbiting any of our solar system’s dwarf planets.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Sonos outlines a plan to win back user trust after its app fiasco
item.description: Speaker company Sonos laid out a number of new plans and commitments on Tuesday in an attempt to turn around the beleaguered sound company following a bug-riddled app relaunch in May that’s still impacting the company.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: NYT Connections today — hints and answers for Wednesday, October 2 (game #479)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency
item.description: Do you know your true cloud costs? The post Why determining the cost of cloud services cuts across an entire agency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Snapchat Accused of Failing to Protect Minors from Sextortion and Child Grooming
item.description: Snap Inc. faced allegations of failing to protect minors on its platform from sextortion, child grooming, and other dangers, according to a complaint filed by New Mexico's attorney general.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Swiss glaciers are receding again after 2 punishing years and despite a good start to 2024
item.description: Experts say the volume of Switzerland’s glaciers shrank again this summer and compounded the negative impact of climate change after a devastating two-year run that depleted the ice by more than 10%.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Rise of Generative AI: Threats and Opportunities for Companies
item.description: Generative AI surpasses human CEOs in decision-making, but poses risks and challenges. Discover the implications for companies and leaders.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FAA confirms SpaceX Falcon 9 is grounded because of Crew-9 launch issue
item.description: The agency is investigating why the rocket's second stage missed its deorbit landing target zone after this weekend's Crew-9 launch.
item.dc:creator: Richard Tribou, The Mercury News

item.title: EU taps AI experts to develop compliance framework for AI regulations
item.description: The European Commission has appointed a group of AI specialists to outline how businesses should comply with forthcoming AI regulations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Inside the trials and tribulations of a major ransomware attack
item.description: Like many small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), ‘JPX Construction’ didn’t see itself as a potential target for a ransomware attack. It wasn’t a high-profile organisation. It didn’t seem to hold much high-value data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Large language models hallucinating non-existent developer packages could fuel supply chain attacks
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have a serious “package hallucination” problem that could lead to a wave of maliciously-coded packages in the supply chain, researchers have discovered in one of the largest and most in-depth ever studies to investigate the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hurricane Helene: Record-Breaking Rainfall Brings Devastation to Southeast US
item.description: Hurricane Helene and a preceding rainstorm dump 40 trillion gallons of water on the Southeast, leading to unprecedented flooding and loss of life.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he's spoken to Musk about getting Starlink for areas damaged by Helene
item.description: Former President Trump on Monday said he’s spoken with Elon Musk about providing Starlink internet service for those without power in states ravaged by Hurricane Helene. Trump visited Valdosta, Ga., where he toured storm damage and expressed support for those dealing with the aftermath.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: California Governor Newsom vetoes AI safety bill, arguing it’s ‘not the best approach’
item.description: In a significant move, California Governor Gavin Newsom vetoed a highly contested artificial intelligence (AI) safety bill on Sunday, citing concerns over its potential to stifle innovation and impose excessive restrictions on AI technologies, including basic functions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft gives enterprises new reasons to adopt Fabric
item.description: Microsoft is working to make its cloud-based Fabric combined offering of data warehousing, data science, data engineering, and data analytics services more attractive, previewing a host of new features at its first annual European Microsoft Fabric Community Conference last week.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Montana man gets 6 months in prison for cloning giant sheep and breeding it
item.description: An 81-year-old Montana has been sentenced to six months in federal prison and ordered to pay $24,000 in penalties for using tissue and testicles from large sheep hunted in Central Asia and the U.S. to illegally create hybrid sheep for captive trophy hunts in Texas and Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Amy Beth Hanson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazil's Supreme Court Orders X to Pay $1.9 Million Fine, Lifting Ban
item.description: Brazilian court fines social platform X $1.9 million, lifting ban on the country
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zero Trust + AI: A match made in the clouds
item.description: Have any terms in the cybersecurity space generated more buzz than “zero trust” and “AI”? At the risk of sounding like a Magic 8 Ball, my reply is no.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NTSB engineer says carbon fiber hull from submersible showed signs of flaws
item.description: The carbon fiber hull of the experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreckage of the Titanic had imperfections dating to the manufacturing process and behaved differently after a loud bang was heard on one of the dives the year before the tragedy, an engineer with the National Transpo
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House cyber workforce bill pushes two-year degrees for gov service
item.description: House Homeland Security Committee Chairman Mark Green's cyber workforce bill adds to a growing push to embrace skills-based hiring and training for cyber jobs. The post House cyber workforce bill pushes two-year degrees for gov service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Infoblox tackles integrated DDI across multi-cloud environments
item.description: Infoblox is rolling out a unified management platform to allow customers to see, control and secure IT resources spread across their hybrid multi-cloud enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DARPA wraps up semifinal of artificial intelligence challenge
item.description: "Inside that synthetic version of the code, we inserted vulnerabilities for the competitors to find," AI Cyber Challenge program manager Andrew Carney said. The post DARPA wraps up semifinal of artificial intelligence challenge first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: X releases its first transparency report since Elon Musk's takeover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Social media platform X on Wednesday published its first transparency report since the company was purchased by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Video game actors' union calls for strike against 'League of Legends'
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — “League of Legends” is caught in the middle of a dispute between Hollywood’s actors union and an audio company that provides voiceover services for the blockbuster online multiplayer game.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top 4 reasons you might still need VPN with cellular service
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains why you may still need a virtual private network even if you're using your phone's cellular network.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
